findings: After FP32 delete + empty_cache: GPU0 549.84W GPU1 574.84W at 0 pct util
549-574W at 0 pct util — most extreme ghost power spike observed across all architectures
Sustained ghost monitoring 600s: GPU0 196-203W GPU1 202-203W at 0 pct util and 728 MB
728 MB VRAM residual throughout 600 second monitoring
NVML utility shows 0 pct throughout — NVML is blind to actual B200 compute activity
Power never returns to 144W baseline — permanently 196-202W

security_issues.type: Extreme ghost power spike — 549-574W at 0 pct util
security_issues.description: Immediately after FP32 process exits: GPU0 = 549.84W, GPU1 = 574.84W at 0 pct reported utilization. Most extreme ghost power event recorded across all 7 architectures. NVML completely blind to this activity.
security_issues.comparison: A100 max desync 357W — B200 exceeds by 217W

security_issues.description: NVML utility.gpu reports 0 pct during what appears to be active compute on B200. Cannot trust NVML for B200 monitoring. Makes conventional monitoring tools useless for B200.
728 MB persists through 600 second ghost monitoring. Does not self-clear.

conclusion: B200 most dangerous architecture tested. 549-574W ghost spike at 0 pct NVML util. NVML completely blind. 728 MB permanent residual. No recovery state.
